insight.headline: Storefront API Update
insight.what_changed: The Storefront API now supports the @inContext directive with an optional channelId argument, allowing developers to apply a specific sales channel's context to an entire query. This change is available in Storefront API version 2026-10. The @inContext directive can be used to fetch data specific to a particular sales channel, such as online store or point of sale.
insight.developer_impact: Developers can use this new feature to build more customized and channel-specific experiences for merchants. To take advantage of this update, developers should update their Storefront API version to 2026-10 and modify their queries to include the channelId argument. This change can be particularly useful for developers building Hydrogen or Remix/React Router apps, as it allows for more flexible and dynamic data fetching. Additionally, developers should review their existing code to ensure compatibility with the updated Storefront API.
insight.merchant_impact: Merchants can expect more tailored and relevant experiences across different sales channels, leading to improved customer engagement and sales. This update enables developers to build more sophisticated and channel-specific features, which can enhance the overall merchant experience.
insight.why_it_matters: This update provides more flexibility and customization options for developers, enabling them to build more sophisticated and channel-specific experiences for merchants. By leveraging the @inContext directive with channelId, developers can create more dynamic and relevant experiences for customers, ultimately driving business growth for merchants.

also_worth_knowing.text: Card deposit endpoint now requires mTLS certificate

updates.title: Physical inventory feature preview
updates.summary: The physical inventory feature preview is available in the unstable version of the GraphQL Admin API, allowing developers to build and test against new inventory APIs.
The Shopify CLI has been updated to version 4.5.2, which includes fixes for theme commands and bumps the Shopify/theme-tools packages.

updates.title: Card deposit endpoint now requires mTLS certificate
updates.summary: The card deposit endpoint now requires a Shopify-issued mTLS client certificate, affecting apps that store cardholder data.

updates.title: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.2
updates.summary: The @shopify/cli-hydrogen package has been updated to version 13.0.2, adding non-interactive shop and storefront creation flags and a force flag to bypass confirmation prompts.

updates.title: Updated App Store requirements: 4.1.2 Use a unique name for your app
updates.summary: The App Store requirements have been updated to include a unique name for each app, reducing merchant confusion over identical app names.

updates.title: Storefront API `@inContext` supports `channelId`
updates.summary: The Storefront API now supports the @inContext directive with an optional channelId argument, allowing developers to apply a specific sales channel's context to an entire query.

updates.title: `productId`, `title`,`variantSku`, and `variantTitle` fields added to `ExchangeLineItem`
updates.summary: The ExchangeLineItem type has been updated to include productId, title, variantSku, and variantTitle fields.

updates.title: POS UI Extensions 2026-07 adds discount allocations to bundle components
updates.summary: The POS UI Extensions API version 2026-07 now includes discount allocation details for product bundle components in cart line item data.
The Shopify CLI has been updated to version 4.5.1, which includes a bump to cli-hydrogen version 13.0.1.
The Shopify CLI version 4.5.0 introduces new store commands, including listing and store creation.

updates.title: @shopify/cli-hydrogen@13.0.1
updates.summary: The @shopify/cli-hydrogen package has been updated to version 13.0.1, which includes improvements to command metadata and build error handling.

updates.title: @shopify/create-hydrogen@5.0.38
updates.summary: The @shopify/create-hydrogen package has been updated to version 5.0.38, which includes an explicit app alias and improvements to JavaScript project configuration.

recap.headline: Shopify updates for GraphQL Admin API, Hydrogen, and Storefront API

themes.title: New Features and Updates
themes.detail: The physical inventory feature preview is available in the unstable version of the GraphQL Admin API, and the Storefront API `@inContext` directive now supports `channelId`. Additionally, the `ExchangeLineItem` type has been updated with new fields, and POS UI Extensions now include discount allocations to bundle components.

themes.title: Hydrogen and CLI Updates
themes.detail: New versions of `@shopify/cli-hydrogen` and `@shopify/create-hydrogen` have been released, with features such as non-interactive shop and storefront creation flags, and improved error handling. The Shopify CLI has also been updated with new store commands and bug fixes.

themes.title: Security and App Requirements
themes.detail: The card deposit endpoint now requires a Shopify-issued mTLS client certificate, and apps must use a unique name to reduce merchant confusion. Apps that store cardholder data must be updated to use the new certificate.

actions.text: Enable physical inventory feature preview on a development store
Update apps to use the new mTLS client certificate for card deposit endpoint
Review and update app names to comply with requirement 4.1.2
